name: Red Bell Pepper and Coriander Pancakes with Guacamole
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp red pepper flakes, or to taste
Salt and freshly ground pepper, to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine flour, cornmeal, red pepper flakes, milk, red bell pepper, cilantro, egg yolks, and melted butter. Season with salt and pepper.
2. Using an electric mixer, beat egg whites until they reach soft peaks. Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the batter.
3. In a nonstick pan over moderate heat, brush some melted butter onto the surface. Drop spoonfuls of batter into the pan so that the pancakes are about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
4. When bubbles form on the surface of the pancakes and their undersides turn golden brown, flip the pancakes using a spatula. Allow the undersides to cook until golden brown.
5. Remove the cooked pancakes to a rack away from direct heat.
Guacamole:
1 avocado, peeled and finely diced
2 tbsp lime juice, or to taste
1 jalapeno, seeded and finely diced
2 tbsp chopped cilantro leaves, or to taste
Salt and freshly ground pepper, to taste
3 Roma tomatoes, seeded and finely diced
1. In a bowl, combine the avocado, lime juice, jalapeno, cilantro, salt, and pepper.
2. If desired, mash the mixture with a fork until smooth.
3. Add the diced tomatoes and gently fold them into the guacamole.
Assembly:
When the pancakes are cool, spoon about 1/2 teaspoon of sour cream onto the center. Top each pancake with an equal amount of guacamole. Garnish with a leaf of cilantro.
Yield: 36 - 40 pancakes
